channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Harris will travel to Asia, Mideast and Europe during her final week in office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris plans to close out her term with an around-the-world trip making stops in Singapore, Bahrain and Germany, her office said. The trip, which is scheduled to last from Jan. 13 to Jan. 17, will be a final opportunity for Harris to address U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US shifts more than $100M in military aid from Israel and Egypt to Lebanon to bolster ceasefire
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration in its final days is shifting more than $100 million in military aid from Israel and Egypt to Lebanon as it tries to bolster a ceasefire agreement it helped mediate between Israel and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks release of special counsel report on Trump cases as court fight simmers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday temporarily blocked the public release of special counsel Jack Smith's report on investigations into Donald Trump as an appeals court weighs a challenge to the disclosure of a much-anticipated document just days before the president-elect reclaims office.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks release of DOJ report on Trump case
item.description: The Trump administration has argued the release of the report should be left to the incoming White House.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: New labels will help people pick devices less at risk of hacking
item.description: Smart devices like baby monitors, fitness trackers and internet-connected appliances could soon carry labels certifying that they meet federal cybersecurity standards.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump refuses to rule out use of military force to take control of Greenland and the Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he won't rule out the use of military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland as he declared U.S. control of both to be vital to American national security.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden administration set to announce 'substantial' final weapons package for Ukraine
item.description: The Biden administration is set to announce a massive, final weapons aid package for Ukraine as part of Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin’s visit to Germany this week to meet with representatives of about 50 partner nations who have come to Ukraine’s defense since Russia invaded nearly three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump announces $20B US investment by Emirati businessman
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is expected to announce a $20 billion investment into building data centers in the United States by the Emirati company led by a billionaire who is a close business partner of the Trump family.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Appeals court rejects Trump's latest attempt to get Friday's hush money sentencing called off
item.description: A New York appeals court judge has denied President-elect Donald Trump’s latest bid to delay this week’s sentencing in his hush money case.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak And Jennifer Peltz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Georgia GOP expels former Lt. Gov. Geoff Duncan from party, citing alleged disloyalty
item.description: The Georgia Republican Party’s State Executive Committee has voted to expel former GOP Lt. Gov. Geoff Duncan from being associated with the party over his alleged disloyalty.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden will announce the creation of two new national monuments to protect tribal lands in California
item.description: The White House says President Joe Biden will establish two new national monuments in California that will honor Native American tribes.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long And Matthew Daly, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Donald Trump Jr. to visit Greenland as his father muses anew about the US taking control of it
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Donald Trump Jr. is heading to Greenland, visiting the Danish territory as his father has continued to stoke seemingly far-fetched suggestions that the U.S. could take control of it.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Office of Cannabis Management will get new interim director
item.description: The interim director of the Minnesota Office of Cannabis Management, who has led the office since it was created, is stepping down.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt in Georgia election workers case
item.description: A federal judge held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt on Monday for failing to comply with court orders in two former Georgia election workers’ efforts to collect their $146 million defamation judgment. At the end of a two-day hearing that saw Giuliani take the stand, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Canada's Trudeau announces resignation after nearly a decade as prime minister
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday after nearly a decade in power, bowing to rising discontent over his leadership and growing turmoil within his government signaled by the abrupt departure of his finance minister.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: O'Malley touts Congressional Black Caucus members in DNC chair bid
item.description: “They are proven winners, and together, we will reengage, reconnect, and rebuild our party,” Former Maryland Gov. Martin O’Malley (D) said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: US eases restrictions on Syria while keeping sanctions in place
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. on Monday eased some restrictions on Syria's transitional government to allow the entry of humanitarian aid after Islamist insurgents ousted Syrian leader Bashar Assad last month. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pence: 'Admirable' that Harris certified Trump's win after losing election
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence, who had to be evacuated four years ago Monday as rioters stormed the Capitol, welcomed "the return of order and civility" as Congress moved to certify President-elect Donald Trump's 2024 victory.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump lawyers push to halt Jan. 10 hush money case sentencing
item.description: President-elect Trump’s legal team urged a judge to hold off on sentencing him on Jan. 10 on his New York criminal conviction ahead of his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Muslim, Arab voters face uncertain political future under Trump
item.description: Muslim and Arab American voters in the U.S. face an uncertain political future as President-elect Trump prepares to take office later this month.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Biden blocks some new offshore drilling ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: President Biden has blocked new offshore drilling for oil and gas in several parts of the country about two weeks before President-elect Trump takes office. Biden has announced that he’ll block new drilling off the entire East Coast, as well as California, Oregon and Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Wisconsin’s Evers revives plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws
item.description: Wisconsin Gov. Tony Evers is reviving his plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws, this time through the state budget.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump is certified as the 2024 election winner without challenge, in stark contrast to 2021 violence
item.description: Lawmakers convened under heavy security and a snowstorm to certify the vote, with the reading of the electoral results from the states coming swiftly.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Harris readies to certify Trump’s election win – and her decisive defeat
item.description: Kamala Harris is readying on Monday to certify the 2024 election results, affirming President-elect Trump’s victory as well as her decisive defeat to him after a whirlwind run for commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Higher Social Security payments coming for millions of people from bill that Biden signed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Sunday signed into law a measure that boosts Social Security payments for current and former public employees, affecting nearly 3 million people who receive pensions from their time as teachers, firefighters, police officers and in other public service jobs.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump appears with Italian Prime Minister Meloni at his Florida club
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump made an appearance Saturday with Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, who was visiting his Mar-a-Lago club.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson faces tough road on Trump agenda after dramatic Speaker vote
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) kept his gavel amid a threat from the right flank of the House GOP, but managing the House GOP is not going to get any easier from here.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: 23-year-old hopeful files to run for Tampa mayor
item.description: The next mayoral race is not until 2027. For Alan Henderson, he hopes that’s enough time for people to know his name and what he hopes to accomplish, if elected.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nathan Fletcher's accuser dismisses sexual assault allegations from lawsuit
item.description: The former Metropolitan Transit System employee who accused Nathan Fletcher of sexual misconduct in a bombshell lawsuit that propelled his resignation from office has walked back some of her most serious allegations in new court filings.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he'll attend Jimmy Carter's funeral
item.description: Asked by The Associated Press as he walked into a New Year's Eve party at his Mar-a-Lago club in Florida, Trump said "I'll be there.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: New year, new laws: These laws are now in effect in Minnesota
item.description: More than a dozen new laws go into effect Jan. 1. The state will see more protections for concert ticket buyers, salary ranges required in job postings, additional rights for renters and more.
item.dc:creator: Anna Haecherl and Anika Besst, MPR News
